title: PodJob — control-plane→pod job envelope (GET /pod/job)
description: What the pod's long-poll returns: one unit of work, dispatched on type. request is an InferRequest (infer_request.schema.json); spec is a RenderSpec (spec.schema.json); chain is an OpChain — a DAG of declared operations (op.schema.json + ops/*.json) run on ONE box with local-disk handoff between steps. Each carries its own version const (infer_version/spec_version/chain_version) — this envelope has none, contracts/VERSION stays the single pin for all three. Adding `ops` is ADDITIVE: every envelope that validated before still validates unchanged, which is why it needs no VERSION bump — examples/pod_job.infer.json and pod_job.render.json are the regression goldens for exactly that.

request.description: Present iff type=infer. Opaque here — validated against infer_request.schema.json.

spec.description: Present iff type=render. Opaque here — validated against spec.schema.json.

chain.description: Present iff type=ops. Opaque here — each step's params are validated against that op's own schema fragment in ops/<op>.json, read by BOTH sides. Deliberately not expanded inline: a per-op block here would be exactly the hand-mirrored surface the op registry exists to abolish.
